questions.text: The osmotic pressure of equimolar solutions of BaCl2, NaCl and sucrose will be in the order:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Osmotic pressure is a colligative property, and the more the number of particles more the osmotic pressure. π=iCRT Hence, BaCl2>NaCl>Sucrose since the concentration is the same and i for BaCl2 is 3, NaCl is 2 and sucrose is 1.

questions.text: Consider NaCl produced in the equation: FeCl3+ NaOH → Fe(OH)3 + NaCl 175.5g of NaCl was dissolved in water to make a liter of solution, the molarity would be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Molarity = number of moles/volume in dm3 or liters 58.5g make up 1 mole of NaCl. 175.5g make up 3 moles of NaCI: 175.5/58.5 = 3 mol That means 3 mole of NaCl is dissolved in 1 liter of solution that makes the molarity of NaCl solution 3M.

questions.explanation: Molarity = number of moles/volume in dm3 or liters 175.5 g make up 3 moles of NaCl 175.5/58.5 = 3 mol That means 3 mole of NaCl is dissolved in 1 liter of solution that makes the NaCl solution 3M.

questions.text: An azeotropic mixture of two liquids has a boiling point higher than either of them when it:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Shows positive deviation from Raoult’s law
options.explanation: In positive deviation, the partial pressure of each component that is present in the solution is greater than the vapour pressure as expected or they have a lower boiling point than the expected value. In this type of deviation, the bond between solute-solvent is weaker than the bond present between solute-solute and solvent-solvent. So, it is easy for them to break the bonds at lower temperatures and hence, have a lower boiling point. The azeotropic solutions which show positive deviation are known as a minimum boiling azeotrope.

options.text: Shows negative deviation from Raoult’s law
options.explanation: Negative deviations are those in which the partial vapour pressure of each component of a solution is less than the expected value of it. For example, the solution of acetone and chloroform. The bond between solute-solvent is stronger than the bond present between solute-solute and solvent-solvent. So, it is difficult for them to break the bonds at lower temperatures and hence, have a high boiling point. The azeotropic solutions which show negative deviation are known as a maximum boiling azeotrope.
Whereas the solution which obeys Roult's law is known as the ideal solution and does not show any deviation in the vapour pressure and boiling point.
A solution in which the maximum amount of solvent has been dissolved. Any more solute added will sit as crystals on the bottom of the container.

questions.explanation: An azeotropic solution is a solution that contains two liquids and has a constant vapour and liquid composition. The azeotropic solutions cannot be separated with the help of the distillation process. They are divided into two parts i.e. positive and negative deviation. Roult's law states that the vapour pressure of a solution that contains a non-volatile solute is directly proportional to the mole fraction of the solvent.

questions.text: The number of moles of solute dissolved per liter of solution is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Normality is described as the number of gram or mole equivalents of solute present in one litre of a solution.
Molality is defined as the “total moles of a solute contained in a kilogram of a solvent.” Molality is also known as molal concentration.•Molality=moles of solute/kg of solvent.

In the solution, the relative amount of solute and solvents are measured by the mole fraction and it is represented by “X.” The mole fraction is the number of moles of a specific component in the solution divided by the total number of moles in the given solution.
Molarity is numer of moles of solute dissolved in liters of solution. So, it is correct option.•molarity = moles of solute / liters of solution.
%COMPOSITION:The ratio of the amount of each element to the total amount of individual elements present in the compound multiplied by 100.•(mass of element/molecular mass) x 100

questions.explanation: Molarity (M) indicates the number of moles of solute per liter of solution.Molality is a property of a solution and is defined as the number of moles of solute per kilogram of solvent. Mole fraction is defined as the ratio of the number of moles of one component of a solution or other mixture to the total number of moles representing all of the moles in solution. Normality is described as the number of gram or mole equivalents of solute present in one liter of a solution. The percent composition of a component in a compound is the percent of the total mass of the compound that is due to that component.

questions.text: From a mixture of CO2 and H2 gases, CO2 can be separated by passing the mixture through:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Carbon dioxide is separated from hydrogen by passing the mixture through the water under high pressure. When carbon dioxide is compressed into water at pressure, the CO2 gas dissolves into the liquid. When pressure is released, CO2 releases from the liquid as a gas in the form of gas bubbles. This process is also called carbonation and is the basis of many popular beverages. Since hydrogen will not dissolve in the water, the two gases will be effectively separated. Carbon dioxide will not dissolve in the forms of water given in all the remaining options, so separation can not occur, leaving only option B as the correct option.

questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide is separated from hydrogen by passing the mixture through the water under high pressure. When carbon dioxide is compressed into water at pressure, the CO2 gas dissolves into the liquid. When pressure is released, CO2 releases from the liquid as a gas in the form of gas bubbles. This process is also called carbonation and is the basis of many popular beverages. Since hydrogen will not dissolve in the water, the two gases will be effectively separated. Carbon dioxide will not dissolve in the forms of water given in all the remaining options, so separation can not occur, leaving only option B as the correct option.

questions.text: The solubility product for BaSO4, at 18-25°C is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The solubility product for BaSO4 represents the equilibrium constant for its dissolution reaction. At 18-25°C, BaSO4 has a very low solubility, resulting in a small concentration of Ba²+ and SO4²- ions in solution, which yields a solubility product of 1×10¹⁰mol²dm-6.

questions.explanation: The solubility product for BaSO4 represents the equilibrium constant for its dissolution reaction. At 18-25°C, BaSO4 has a very low solubility, resulting in a small concentration of Ba²+ and SO4²- ions in solution, which yields a solubility product of 1×10¹⁰mol²dm-6.

questions.text: 6 g of urea is dissolved in 90 g of water. The relative lowering of vapour pressure is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The relative lowering of pressure (π) is given by the equation: π = (number of moles of solute / number of moles of solvent) x (RT / P) where R is the gas constant, T is the temperature in Kelvin, and P is the vapor pressure of the solvent. First, we need to calculate the number of moles of urea (CH4N2O): Mass of urea = 6 g Molar mass of urea = 60 g/mol Number of moles of urea = 6 g / 60 g/mol = 0.1 mol Next, we need to calculate the vapor pressure of water (P): At 25°C, the vapor pressure of water is 23.76 mmHg. Now we can calculate the relative lowering of pressure: π = (0.1 / 18) x (0.082 x 298 / 23.76) π = 0.0195 or 0.02 (rounded to two significant figures) Therefore, the relative lowering of pressure is 0.02. Answer is a.

questions.explanation: This is the following solution.

questions.text: 0.5 molar solution NaOH contains:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Calcuation suggests D is correct
Calculation suggests D is correct
Calculation suggests D is correct

Atomic mass of Na: 23g/mol Atomic mass of O: 16g/mol Atomic mass of H: 1g/mol Molar mass of NaOH: 23+6+1= 40 g/mol Molarity= number of moles= 0.5 For a solution to be one molar, one mole of a substance has to be dissolved in 1dm3 of the solvent. To find out the mass of NaOH, we will use; No.of moles= mass/ molar mass 0.5= m/40 m= 40x0.5 m= 20g

questions.explanation: The atomic mass of Na is 23g/mol, the atomic mass of O is 16g/mol, and the atomic mass of H is 1g/mol so, by adding all three atomic masses, we can obtain the mass number of NaOH, which is 23+16+1=40g/mol. For a solution to be 1 molar, 1 mole of a substance has to be dissolved in 1 dm3 of the solvent. Since we have to find out the mass of NaOH needed to make a 0.5 molar solution, we will dissolve 0.5 moles of NaOH in 1 dm3 of the solvent. 0.5 mol of NaOH corresponds to 20g of NaOH which can be worked out using the proportion method that if 1 mol of NaOH contains 40g of NaOH (as calculated above) then 0.5 mol of NaOH will contain 20g of NaOH. If the proportion method seems difficult, the formula relating mass with moles can be used which is: n=m/M; where M is molar mass, n is the number of moles, and m is the mass of NaOH for n moles. (0.5) = m/(40) m=(40)(0.5)=20g.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: A solution freezes at a lower temperature than the pure solvent
options.explanation: Upon the addition of a solute which is non-volatile, the vapor pressure of the solution is found to be lower than the vapor pressure of the pure solvent. This causes the solid and the solution to reach equilibrium at lower temperatures. So solution freezes at lower temperature than pure solvent.

options.text: A solution boils at a higher temperature than the pure solvent.
options.explanation: The boiling point of a solution will be greater than the boiling point of the pure solvent because the solution (which has a lower vapor pressure) will need to be heated to a higher temperature in order for the vapor pressure to become equal to the external pressure (i.e., the boiling point).

options.text: 0.1 M NaCl solution and 0.1 m sugar solution have the same boiling point
options.explanation: Molarity is the moles of solute in 1 litre of solution Molality is the moles of solute per 1 kg of solvent. Therefore, a molal solution is less concentrated than a molar solution because the latter contains less solvent. The answer is C as the elevation of the boiling point depends on the number of particles added to the solution. Since we have NaCl being ionic unlike sugar, NaCl ionizes to give Na ions and Cl ions, doubling the number of particles in the solution, leading to a further elevation in the boiling point.

options.text: Osmosis cannot take place without a semi-permeable membrane.
options.explanation: Osmosis only occurs when a semi- permeable membrane is present, but diffusion can happen whether or not it is present. Osmosis only allows solvent molecules to move freely, but diffusion allows both solvent and solute molecules to move freely.

questions.explanation: Molarity is the moles of solute in 1 litre of solution Molality is the moles of solute per 1 kg of solvent. Therefore, a molal solution is less concentrated than a molar solution because the latter contains less solvent. The answer is C as the elevation of the boiling point depends on the number of particles added to the solution. Since we have NaCl being ionic unlike sugar, NaCl ionizes to give Na ions and Cl ions, doubling the number of particles in the solution, leading to a further elevation in the boiling point.

questions.text: The value of surface tension of isopropyl alcohol is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The intermolecular forces of water are more than that of isopropyl alcohol, and as surface tension depends on intermolecular forces, so water has more surface tension than isopropyl alcohol.
This option is correct because the intermolecular forces of isopropyl alcohol are greater than that of ethylene glycol hence, greater surface tension.
Isopropyl alcohol has a lower surface tension than methyl alcohol due to the stronger cohesive forces in isopropyl alcohol.
Isopropyl alcohol generally has a slightly higher surface tension than ethyl alcohol due to the presence of larger molecular groups, leading to stronger intermolecular forces.

questions.explanation: Surface tension depends mainly upon the forces of attraction between the particles within the given liquid and also upon the gas, solid, or liquid in contact with it. The stronger the intermolecular interactions, the greater the surface tension. To answer this question, you have to compare the intermolecular forces between the molecules by comparing their structures. Structure of isopropyl alcohol: since it has 1 OH group, it can form 1 hydrogen bond maximum. Structure of water: water can form 4 hydrogen bonds maximum. Structure of ethylene glycol: ethylene glycol has 2 OH groups, so it can form 2 Hydrogen bonds at maximum. It also has less wander vaal forces than isopropyl alcohol due to its lesser size and Mr. Structure of Methyl Alcohol: It can form 1 Hydrogen bond at maximum like isopropyl alcohol but has less Vander waal forces than isopropyl alcohol as it has a small size and less Mr so will have more surface tension.

questions.text: The properties of liquid crystals are intermediate between crystals and:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Liquid crystals are anisotropic fluids, and have properties which are an intermediate between crystals and isotropic liquids.

questions.text: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes to other is called _ temperature:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes into another is called the transition temperature or phase transition temperature. This process is commonly observed in materials undergoing a phase transition, such as from a solid to another solid polymorphic form. The specific terms for these transitions can vary depending on the nature of the substance, for example, it could be called a melting point, freezing point, or transition temperature.

questions.explanation: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes into another is called the transition temperature or phase transition temperature. This process is commonly observed in materials undergoing a phase transition, such as from a solid to another solid polymorphic form. The specific terms for these transitions can vary depending on the nature of the substance, for example, it could be called a melting point, freezing point, or transition temperature.

questions.text: Which statement is correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Glucagon is secreted by the b-­cells of islets of Langerhans and stimulates glycogenolysis.
Secretion of thymosins is stimulated with aging
In females, FSH first binds with specific receptors on ovarian cell membrane
options.explanation: Hormone action involves their reception by target cells. Specific proteins called hormone receptors that are located in target tissues only bind with these hormones. Hormone receptor may be of two types: membrane-bound receptor and intracellular receptors. Steroid hormones etc., bind with intracellular receptors while some hormones e.g., pituitary hormones like FSH etc., bind with membrane-bound receptors.

options.text: FSH stimulates the secretion of estrogen and progesterone.
options.explanation: FSH stimulates the production of estrogen which in turn inhibits FSH. Estrogen stimulates Progesterone which in turn inhibits estrogen.

questions.explanation: Hormone action involves their reception by target cells. Specific proteins called hormone receptors that are located in target tissues only bind with these hormones. Hormone receptor may be of two types: membrane-bound receptor and intracellular receptors. Steroid hormones etc., bind with intracellular receptors while some hormones e.g., pituitary hormones like FSH etc., bind with membrane-bound receptors.

questions.text: Aqueous solutions of Iodine and Sodium hydroxide were mixed in a round bottom flask at 70 C. Following chemical reaction was carried out. 3I2 + 6 NaOH → NaIO3 + NaI + H2OThis reaction is termed as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: To determine whether such a reaction is a redox reaction, the oxidation states of the reactants and products is compared. The oxidation state of Na remains +1 throughout, as does the oxidation states of Hydrogen (+1) and Oxygen (-2). Iodine undergoes a change in oxidation state. Its oxidation state is 0, in its molecular form, on the reactant side. In NaI, iodine's oxidation state is -1 so, Iodine has undergone reduction. In NaIO3, the oxidation states of Na remain +1 and oxygen's also remains -2 so, we can determine that the oxidation state of iodine is +5 in this reaction. (NOTE: For oxidation numbers, the total oxidation state of a compound is zero so, Iodine's oxidation number must be +5 to ensure NaIO3’s oxidation state is zero). Since Iodine underwent oxidation AND reduction, we can conclude that this is a redox reaction (disproportionation to be precise, as the same species has undergone oxidation and reduction) so, option A is correct.
Option B is incorrect, as the two salts produced are soluble in water.
Option C does not describe this reaction.
Option D is incorrect as no free radicals are produced during this reaction.

questions.explanation: To determine whether such a reaction is a redox reaction, the oxidation states of the reactants and products is compared. The oxidation state of Na remains +1 throughout, as does the oxidation states of Hydrogen (+1) and Oxygen (-2). Iodine undergoes a change in oxidation state. Its oxidation state is 0, in its molecular form, on the reactant side. In NaI, iodine's oxidation state is -1 so, Iodine has undergone reduction. In NaIO3, the oxidation states of Na remain +1 and oxygen's also remains -2 so, we can determine that the oxidation state of iodine is +5 in this reaction. (NOTE: For oxidation numbers, the total oxidation state of a compound is zero so, Iodine's oxidation number must be +5 to ensure NaIO3’s oxidation state is zero). Since Iodine underwent oxidation AND reduction, we can conclude that this is a redox reaction (disproportionation to be precise, as the same species has undergone oxidation and reduction) so, option A is correct. Option B is incorrect, as the two salts produced are soluble in water. Option C does not describe this reaction. Option D is incorrect as no free radicals are produced during this reaction.

questions.text: Buffer solutions resist change in their?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because buffers can't resist change in their Temperature.
This option is incorrect because buffers can't resist change in their Solubility.
This option is incorrect because buffers can't resist change in their Volatility.
This option is correct because a buffer is a solution that can resist pH change upon the addition of an acidic or basic component.

questions.explanation: Correct option is D. A buffer is a solution that can resist pH change upon the addition of an acidic or basic component. It is able to neutralize small amounts of added acid or base, thus maintaining the pH of the solution relatively stable. This is important for processes and/or reactions which require specific and stable pH ranges.

questions.text: Elevation of the boiling point is a _ property:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Boiling point elevation is a colligative property which means it depends on the presence and the number of dissolved particles and not on the solute's identity.

questions.text: For a chemical reaction to occur, particles that collide should:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The collision theory states that for particles to take part in a chemical reaction, they must collide with one another, have sufficient energies, proper orientation, and should be in a homogenous mixture.

questions.text: 10.0 grams of glucose are dissolved in water to make 100 cm3 of its solution, its molarity is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: 0.55: This option suggests a molarity of 0.55 for the glucose solution. To determine the molarity, we need to calculate the number of moles of glucose present in the solution. Given that the mass of glucose is 10.0 grams and its molar mass is approximately 180.16 g/mol, we can calculate the number of moles using the formula:Number of moles = Mass of substance / Molar massNumber of moles of glucose = 10.0 g / 180.16 g/mol ≈ 0.0554 molNow, we can calculate the molarity using the formula:Molarity = Number of moles / Volume of solution (in liters)Since the volume of the solution is given as 100 cm3, we need to convert it to liters:Volume of solution = 100 cm3 / 1000 = 0.1 LMolarity = 0.0554 mol / 0.1 L ≈ 0.554 M

0.1: This option suggests a molarity of 0.1 for the glucose solution. However, the calculated molarity in option a) is approximately 0.554 M, which is not equal to 0.1 M. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

10: This option suggests a molarity of 10 for the glucose solution. Since molarity is the number of moles of solute per liter of solution, a molarity of 10 would mean that the number of moles of glucose is 10 times the volume of the solution, which is not possible. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

1: This option suggests a molarity of 1 for the glucose solution. However, the calculated molarity in option a) is approximately 0.554 M, which is not equal to 1 M. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: 0.55: This option suggests a molarity of 0.55 for the glucose solution. To determine the molarity, we need to calculate the number of moles of glucose present in the solution. Given that the mass of glucose is 10.0 grams and its molar mass is approximately 180.16 g/mol, we can calculate the number of moles using the formula:Number of moles = Mass of substance / Molar massNumber of moles of glucose = 10.0 g / 180.16 g/mol ≈ 0.0554 molNow, we can calculate the molarity using the formula:Molarity = Number of moles / Volume of solution (in liters)Since the volume of the solution is given as 100 cm3, we need to convert it to liters:Volume of solution = 100 cm3 / 1000 = 0.1 LMolarity = 0.0554 mol / 0.1 L ≈ 0.554 M

This is not the correct presentation for Ksp.
This is not the correct presentation for Ksp.

questions.explanation: The solubility product constant, Ksp , is the equilibrium constant for a solid substance dissolving in an aqueous solution. It represents the level at which a solute dissolves in solution. The more soluble a substance is, the higher the Ksp value it has.

questions.text: As the number of solute particles increases, the freezing point of the solution:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option suggests that the freezing point of the solution does not change with an increase in the number of solute particles. However, this is not accurate. The freezing point of a solution does change when solute particles are added.

When a solute is dissolved in a solvent, the freezing point of the solution decreases compared to the pure solvent. This happens because the solute particles disrupt the crystal lattice structure of the solvent, making it more difficult for the solvent to freeze. The extent to which the freezing point is lowered depends on the concentration of the solute particles in the solution.

This option suggests that the freezing point of the solution initially increases and then decreases as the number of solute particles increases. In reality, the freezing point of a solution always deccreases with the addition of solute particles.

When a solute is dissolved in a solvent, the freezing point of the solution decreases compared to the pure solvent. This happens because the solute particles disrupt the crystal lattice structure of the solvent, making it more difficult for the solvent to freeze. The extent to which the freezing point is lowered depends on the concentration of the solute particles in the solution.According to the Colligative property, the change in freezing point of a solution is directly proportional to the molality of the solution, which is the number of moles of solute per kilogram of solvent. Thus, as the number of solute particles increases, the molality of the solution increases, and so does the degree of depression of the freezing point. As a result, the freezing point of the solution lowers.

questions.explanation: When a solute is dissolved in a solvent, the freezing point of the solution decreases compared to the pure solvent. This happens because the solute particles disrupt the crystal lattice structure of the solvent, making it more difficult for the solvent to freeze. The extent to which the freezing point is lowered depends on the concentration of the solute particles in the solution.According to the Colligative property, the change in freezing point of a solution is directly proportional to the molality of the solution, which is the number of moles of solute per kilogram of solvent. Thus, as the number of solute particles increases, the molality of the solution increases, and so does the degree of depression of the freezing point. As a result, the freezing point of the solution lowers.

questions.text: Boiling point constants help us to determine:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Boiling point elevation is a colligative property that depends on the number of solute particles in a solution. The boiling point constant, Kb, is a proportionality constant that relates the molality of a solution to the elevation in boiling point.The equation for boiling point elevation is:ΔTb = Kb x molalitywhere ΔTb is the change in boiling point, Kb is the boiling point constant, and molality is the concentration of the solute in mol/kg of the solvent.By measuring the change in boiling point and knowing the boiling point constant of the solvent, we can calculate the molality of the solution. Then, using the molality and mass of the solute, we can calculate the molar mass of the solute. Therefore, boiling point constants help us to determine molar masses of solutes in solutions.
Boiling point constants are not directly related to determining volumes. They are more concerned with changes in boiling points based on the number of solute particles present in a solution.
Boiling point constants are not used to determine pressures. The primary focus of boiling point constants is to understand the change in boiling point due to the presence of solute particles.

This is related to the correct answer. Boiling point constants are used to determine the molar masses of substances, which involve calculating masses based on the change in boiling point caused by the presence of solute particles in a solution.

questions.explanation: Boiling point elevation is a colligative property that depends on the number of solute particles in a solution. The boiling point constant, Kb, is a proportionality constant that relates the molality of a solution to the elevation in boiling point.The equation for boiling point elevation is:ΔTb = Kb x molalitywhere ΔTb is the change in boiling point, Kb is the boiling point constant, and molality is the concentration of the solute in mol/kg of the solvent.By measuring the change in boiling point and knowing the boiling point constant of the solvent, we can calculate the molality of the solution. Then, using the molality and mass of the solute, we can calculate the molar mass of the solute. Therefore, boiling point constants help us to determine molar masses of solutes in solutions.

questions.text: Molal freezing point constant of water is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The molal freezing point constant of water, also known as the cryoscopic constant, is a constant value that relates the change in freezing point of a solvent to the molality of the solute. For water, the molal freezing point constant (Kf) is approximately 1.86°C/m. This means that for every 1 molal (1 mol/kg) concentration of a solute in water, the freezing point of the solution will be lowered by approximately 1.86°C compared to the freezing point of pure water.
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

questions.text: Ca(OH)2 is sparingly soluble having solubility constant value 6.5 x 10-6. What’ll be its solubility:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As per given calculation, x ≈ 0.0117 mol/L.
As per given calculation, x ≈ 0.0117 mol/L.
As per given calculation, x ≈ 0.0117 mol/L.
As per given calculation, x ≈ 0.0117 mol/L.

questions.explanation: The solubility product constant (Ksp) expression for Ca(OH)2is: Ca(OH)2(s) ⇌ Ca2+(aq) + 2OH-(aq) The Ksp expression can be written as: Ksp = [Ca2+][OH-]^2 Given that the solubility constant value (Ksp) is 6.5 x10^-6, we can assume that x mol/L of Ca(OH)2 dissolves, leading to theformation of x mol/L of Ca2+ and 2x mol/L of OH-. Substituting these values into the Ksp expression, we get: 6.5 x 10^-6 = (x)(2x)^2 6.5 x 10^-6 = 4x^3 Dividing both sides by 4, we have: 1.625 x 10^-6 = x^3 To find x, we can take the cube root of both sides: x = (1.625 x 10^-6)^(1/3) Calculating this value gives us: x ≈ 0.0117 mol/L Therefore, the solubility of Ca(OH)2 is approximately 0.0117mol/L.

questions.text: The compound with more than 10% solubility in pure water is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In MgCO3, the lattice energy of Mg++ is more due to its smaller size and more charge hence its solubility will be less.
For Al2(CO3)3, the charge on Al is +3 and its size is also less than that of Mg because atomic size decreases down the group so its lattice energy will be high and so solubility will be less.

Among the given options the size of K+ is the largest and its charge(+1) is lesser than other given compounds due to which its lattice energy will be least and so will have more solubility in pure water.
Due to greater charge and lesser size the lattice energy of Zn++ is more and hence solubility will be less.

questions.explanation: The solubility of compounds in pure water depends upon their respective lattice energy. If lattice energy is larger than the hydration energy i.e. ions are strongly held and the solubility of that compound in water is less. And if lattice energy is less than the hydration energy then the compound is dissociated into ions and hence solubility increases. Lattice energy is directly related to charge density (charge/size).

questions.text: The elevation in boiling point ∆𝑇𝑏 is equal to ebullioscopic constant 𝐾𝑏 when the Molarity (𝑀) of the solution is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to the formula for the elevation of boiling point∆Tb=kb×m Where kb is the ebullioscopic constant and m is the molality of the solution. ∆Tb will be equal to kb only if molality is 1.0m and it has no relation with the molarity of the solution so the given statement is wrong.

questions.text: The aqueous solution of which of the following electrolyte will conduct electric current to large extent:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Strong bases are defined as base which completely dissociates when dissolved in water and is a strong electrolyte. Mg(OH)2 = Mg+2 + 2OH- As it produces 3 ions on dissociation, thus would conduct electricity to the largest extent.
A weak acid is defined as the acid which does not completely dissociate when dissolved in water and is a weak electrolyte. H2CO3⇌2H+ +CO32-
Salt is defined as a substance formed by combination of acid and base, which completely dissociates when dissolved in water and is a strong electrolyte. NH4Cl→NH4+ + Cl-
Weak bases are defined as the basis which does not completely dissociate when dissolved in water and is a weak electrolyte. NH4OH⇌NH4+ +OH-(aq).

questions.explanation: Strong bases are defined as the base which completely dissociates when dissolved in water and is a strong electrolyte. Mg(OH)2 = Mg+2 + 2OH- As it produces 3 ions on dissociation , thus would conduct electricity to the largest extent

questions.text: Which of the following concentration unit is temperature dependent:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Molality of the solution does not change with the temperature of the solution. In case of molality it is the ratio of moles to mass. Mass is the same at any temperature hence molality won't change with change in temperature
Because mass is independent of temperature, and so is the number of particles (i.e. the moles). Mole fraction is the ratio of the moles of one substance to the total moles.

Molarity of a solution depends upon temperature because volume of a solution is temperature dependent. What happens is that when the temperature is increased the distance between the molecules in a liquid increase leading to volume expansion. In turn leading to decrease in molarity. Molarity = Moles of solute / volume of solution

questions.explanation: Molarity of a solution depends upon temperature because volume of a solution is temperature dependent. What happens is that when the temperature is increased the distance between the molecules in a liquid increase leading to volume expansion. In turn leading to decrease in molarity. Molarity = Moles of solute / volume of solution

questions.text: Colloidal particles can be separated by using:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Ordinary filter paperOrdinary filter paper is not suitable for separating colloidal particles. Colloidal particles are very small in size, typically ranging from 1 nanometer (nm) to 1 micrometer (μm). Ordinary filter paper has larger pores that allow particles of colloidal size to pass through, so it will not effectively separate colloidal particles.

b) Coarse filter paperCoarse filter paper also has larger pores, similar to ordinary filter paper. As a result, it will not effectively separate colloidal particles. Colloidal particles are small enough to pass through the pores of coarse filter paper.

c) Fine filter paperFine filter paper has smaller pores compared to ordinary and coarse filter paper. It is designed to trap smaller particles. While it can remove larger particles from a liquid, it may still allow colloidal particles to pass through, as colloids are smaller than what fine filter paper can effectively capture.

Colloidal particles are small solid particles that are suspended in a fluid phase. Their size range is typically between 10nm. Colloidal particles can be separated by a centrifugation method using a fine filter.Colloidal particles cannot be separated by using filter paper, regardless of its porosity, as the size of the particles is too small to be trapped by the paper. Instead, colloidal particles can be separated by techniques such as centrifugation, dialysis, or ultrafiltration, which are specifically designed for the separation of colloids based on their size and charge.

questions.explanation: Colloidal particles are small solid particles that are suspended in a fluid phase. Their size range is typically between 10nm. Colloidal particles can be separated by a centrifugation method using fine filter.

questions.text: The pH of a solution can be kept constant by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. A saturated solution refers to a solution in which the maximum amount of solute has been dissolved at a particular temperature and pressure. In other words, it is a solution that has reached its solubility limit.

This option is incorrect. A supersaturated solution is one that contains more dissolved solute than would normally be possible at a given temperature and pressure. It is achieved by dissolving an excess amount of solute in a solvent at an elevated temperature and then slowly cooling the solution down without allowing the excess solute to precipitate out.

This option is correct. A buffer solution is a solution that resists changes in pH when small amounts of acid or base are added to it. It consists of a mixture of a weak acid and its conjugate base (or a weak base and its conjugate acid). The buffer solution works by undergoing a reversible reaction that consumes or releases hydrogen ions (H+) to maintain a relatively constant pH. The buffer solution responds to the addition of acid or base by shifting the equilibrium of the weak acid-conjugate base pair, minimizing the change in pH. Buffer solutions are used where maintaining a constant pH is critical.

This option is incorrect. A dilute solution is a solution that contains a relatively small amount of solute dissolved in a larger amount of solvent. In other words, the concentration of the solute is relatively low compared to the amount of solvent present.

questions.explanation: A buffer solution is a solution that resists changes in pH when small amounts of acid or base are added to it. It consists of a mixture of a weak acid and its conjugate base (or a weak base and its conjugate acid). The buffer solution works by undergoing a reversible reaction that consumes or releases hydrogen ions (H+) to maintain a relatively constant pH. The buffer solution responds to the addition of acid or base by shifting the equilibrium of the weak acid-conjugate base pair, minimizing the change in pH.Buffer solutions are used where maintaining a constant pH is critical.

questions.text: Le Chaterlier’s principle discussed the effects of following on equilibrium
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Concentration, work, heat - This is incorrect. While concentration and heat are factors discussed by Le Chatelier's principle, "work" is not typically considered as a factor affecting equilibrium
Volume, heat, pressure - This is partially correct but incomplete. While volume and pressure are related factors for gases, concentration and temperature are also significant factors.

Le Chatelier's principle states that if a system at equilibrium is subjected to an external change, the system adjusts itself to counteract that change and restore a new equilibrium. The principle discusses the effects of the following factors on equilibrium:Concentration: If the concentration of reactants or products is changed, the system will shift the equilibrium position to counteract the change and restore a new equilibrium.Pressure: For reactions involving gases, changes in pressure can affect the equilibrium position. If the pressure is increased, the system will shift the equilibrium position to the side with fewer moles of gas, and if the pressure is decreased, it will shift to the side with more moles of gas.Temperature: Changes in temperature can also influence the equilibrium position. For exothermic reactions (where heat is a product), an increase in temperature will shift the equilibrium position to the left (towards the reactants), and for endothermic reactions (where heat is a reactant), an increase in temperature will shift the equilibrium position to the right (towards the products).
This is incorrect. Le Chatelier's principle specifically addresses the effects of concentration, pressure, and temperature on equilibrium.

questions.explanation: Le Chatelier's principle states that if a system at equilibrium is subjected to an external change, the system adjusts itself to counteract that change and restore a new equilibrium. The principle discusses the effects of the following factors on equilibrium:Concentration: If the concentration of reactants or products is changed, the system will shift the equilibrium position to counteract the change and restore a new equilibrium.Pressure: For reactions involving gases, changes in pressure can affect the equilibrium position. If the pressure is increased, the system will shift the equilibrium position to the side with fewer moles of gas, and if the pressure is decreased, it will shift to the side with more moles of gas.Temperature: Changes in temperature can also influence the equilibrium position. For exothermic reactions (where heat is a product), an increase in temperature will shift the equilibrium position to the left (towards the reactants), and for endothermic reactions (where heat is a reactant), an increase in temperature will shift the equilibrium position to the right (towards the products).

questions.text: Pure water is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pure water is a poor conductor of electricity. It has a low concentration of ions, primarily H+ and OH− ions resulting from the autoionization of water. The low ion concentration makes pure water a poor conductor compared to substances with higher ion concentrations, like salts or acids.
This is incorrect because, in reality, pure water is not a very good conductor of electricity. Its low ion concentration limits its conductivity.
This is partially correct, but the term "slight conductance" might be misleading. It's better to emphasize that pure water has low conductivity due to a low concentration of ions

While pure water is neutral in terms of pH, the question is about its conductivity. Neutrality does not necessarily imply high or low conductivity. Pure water's low conductivity is mainly due to its low ion concentration, not its pH

questions.explanation: Pure water is a poor conductor of electricity. It has a low concentration of ions, primarily H+ and OH− ions resulting from the autoionization of water. The low ion concentration makes pure water a poor conductor compared to substances with higher ion concentrations, like salts or acids.

questions.text: The presence of common ions the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The presence of common ions in a solution can affect the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound. This phenomenon is described by Le Chatelier's principle.When a common ion is added to a solution containing a slightly soluble salt, the equilibrium shifts toward the side of the reaction that reduces the concentration of that ion. As a result, the solubility of the ionic compound decreases.For example, if you have a solution of a sparingly soluble salt, say AB, and you add a common ion A+ or B− (which comes from another soluble salt), the equilibrium will shift to the left, decreasing the dissociation of AB and reducing its solubility.Therefore, the correct answer is that the presence of common ions decreases the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound

questions.explanation: The presence of common ions in a solution can affect the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound. This phenomenon is described by Le Chatelier's principle.When a common ion is added to a solution containing a slightly soluble salt, the equilibrium shifts toward the side of the reaction that reduces the concentration of that ion. As a result, the solubility of the ionic compound decreases.For example, if you have a solution of a sparingly soluble salt, say AB, and you add a common ion A+ or B− (which comes from another soluble salt), the equilibrium will shift to the left, decreasing the dissociation of AB and reducing its solubility.Therefore, the correct answer is that the presence of common ions decreases the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound
